AI ads win over millennials, but Gen Z still isn't convinced
Article
by
EMARKETER Editors
|
Mar 12, 2026
Download
AI ads win over millennials, but Gen Z still isn't convinced
Share
Topics
Artificial Intelligence (AI)
Digital Advertising
Generation Z
United States
E-Mail Chart
Download
Key stat:
Gen Z is nearly twice as likely as millennials to view AI-generated ads negatively, 39% vs. 20%, according to January data from the Interactive Advertising Bureau and Sonata Insights.
Beyond the chart:
The Gen Z resistance has a clear alternative: Authenticity. Some
70% of Gen Z consumers say UGC is very helpful to their buying journey, and 60% of consumers overall say UGC is the most genuine form of advertising
, per dcdx.
Meanwhile, the skepticism isn't just a Gen Z phenomenon.
Only 12% of US adults say they're more likely to buy from brands using AI in advertising
, per CivicScience, and 28% are mostly negative about AI in marketing overall, per Tinuiti.
Use this chart:
Drop this in your next creative or media planning meeting to show how differently Gen Z and millennials respond to AI-generated ads. Use the sentiment gap to pressure-test your campaign targeting, and show your brand team where AI creative needs more human polish to earn younger audiences.
Related EMARKETER reports:
Generational Attitudes Toward Digital Advertising 2025
(EMARKETER subscription required)
AI, Consumer Behavior, and the Trust Economy
(EMARKETER subscription required)
Methodology: Data is from the January 2026 Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB) and Sonata Insights report titled "The AI Ad Gap Widens." 505 US consumers ages 16-43 were surveyed during October 2025-January 2026. 150 respondents were Gen Z (ages 16-27) and 150 were millennials (ages 28-43). In addition, 104 US advertising executives at companies with annual media spend of at least $1 million were also surveyed.
We prepared this article with the assistance of generative AI tools and stand behind its accuracy, quality, and originality.
